data.body: The /dso:onboarding skill fails to install required DSO commit hooks (pre-commit-test-gate.sh, pre-commit-review-gate.sh, auto-format) in the host project. When asked whether commit hooks were configured, the agent incorrectly stated these are only Claude Code tool-use hooks and not git commit hooks — but the DSO architecture explicitly uses a two-layer defense-in-depth model where Layer 1 is git pre-commit hooks and Layer 2 is Claude Code PreToolUse hooks. Both layers are required. The agent confused the two layers and skipped Layer 1 entirely. The onboarding skill should: (1) Install pre-commit-test-gate.sh and pre-commit-review-gate.sh as git pre-commit hooks in the host project (integrating with existing hook managers like Husky if present). (2) Configure the auto-format PostToolUse hook. (3) Not treat these as optional or ask whether the user wants them — they are core DSO enforcement infrastructure. The two-layer model exists precisely so that enforcement cannot be bypassed by either git or Claude Code alone.
